testCases.title: Logout - Token Revocation
testCases.notes: POST /revoke/token returns HTTP 200 (logout flow correct). After logout, post-logout fetch to /graphql with the OLD access_token (jti=e8d4fff2) via storefront proxy returned HTTP 200 with the user's data { me: { userName: 'qa-agent-slot1@virtocommerce.com' } }. Bogus token and no-token both correctly return 'Anonymous', proving the Bearer header IS honored — only the actual revoked access token is still accepted. This indicates only the refresh_token is invalidated by /revoke; the access_token remains valid for its full TTL (~30 min). Cross-references suite 044 NOT_TESTED note about token expiry not being exposed. May be by-design OAuth behavior but contradicts BL-AUTH-003 'token revocation must be immediate'. Routes to qa-testing-expert investigation.

testCases.title: Impersonation - View as Customer
testCases.notes: Logged in as admin in Admin SPA, found target customer 'Agent Chrome' (qa-agent-slot1@virtocommerce.com), navigated to user record, located 'Login on behalf' toolbar action. Clicked it — opened storefront URL /account/impersonate/c994fa34-... in new tab which immediately redirected to /403 'Access denied'. Two possible root causes: (1) admin user lacks impersonation permission scope in QA env, (2) single-browser-context cookie collision (storefront tab already had active session for the same user). Cannot resolve in single playwright-chrome browser context. Requires either elevated admin permissions OR isolated incognito browser context — neither available in this run.

Used product bee0d93a (UNTUCKit eGift Card) as TEST_SKU. Cart subtotal $2836 / total $1727.99 preserved across cookie-clear -> re-auth via /sign-in?returnUrl=/cart. Cart count badge 7 matches both before-expiry and after-reauth states.

testCases.title: Concurrent Sessions - Both Active Independently
testCases.notes: Single playwright-chrome browser context cannot simulate two truly isolated browser sessions (tabs share cookies in one context). Requires running in two separate browser servers (e.g., chrome + firefox) or two isolated incognito contexts. Cross-server orchestration is the regression-orchestrator's job, not this single test runner's scope.

testCases.title: Token Replay After Logout - API Rejection
testCases.failedAssertion: [STATE] API call with old token returns 401 Unauthorized
testCases.notes: Re-verified with a fresh token capture (jti=a9db41df). After logout via account-menu popup (POST /revoke/token returned 200), an immediate fetch to /graphql with the revoked Bearer token returned HTTP 200 with the user's data. Confirmed not a session-cookie fallback because no-auth requests on the same /graphql endpoint return 'Anonymous'. Same root cause as AUTH-024: only refresh tokens are invalidated; access tokens remain valid for ~30 min TTL. BL-AUTH-003 violation. P0 candidate per security severity, but typical OAuth design pattern - needs architectural decision.

testCases.title: RBAC - Customer Cannot Access Admin API
testCases.notes: Cross-origin requests from storefront (vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com) to admin REST API (vcst-qa.govirto.com/api/platform/*) are CORS-preflight-blocked at the browser layer (ERR_FAILED before status). Browser cannot directly observe whether admin API rejects customer tokens with 401/403. Defense-in-depth: storefront can't even reach admin API. To validate the actual 401/403 response, this test must run via backend test runner (curl or Postman) - outside the qa-frontend-expert scope. Recommend reroute to qa-backend-expert or graphql-runner with REST helper.

GraphQL { me } without auth returns 'Anonymous' (no real user data). Anonymous mutation attempts return 400 (validation) without privilege escalation. Admin REST endpoints CORS-blocked. Strong RBAC at perimeter.

/account -> /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/dashboard PASS; /account/orders -> /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/orders PASS; /company/members -> /sign-in?returnUrl=/company/members PASS. /checkout -> /cart (NOT /sign-in) - by-design behavior (single-page checkout overlay; cart is publicly accessible; gating happens at Place Order). Test step 3 expectation that /checkout redirects to login is incorrect for this storefront - should be updated to verify /cart redirect or place-order auth gate.
Visited /account/orders unauthenticated -> redirected to /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/orders. After login, lander on /account/orders correctly. Return URL preservation works.

bugs.title: Access token NOT revoked after logout - token replay possible until natural TTL expiry
bugs.stepsToReproduce:
1. Sign in to storefront as qa-agent-slot1@virtocommerce.com / TestAgent1!
2. Capture access_token from POST /connect/token response (e.g., jti=a9db41df, exp 1778004722)
3. Click user name in top header -> click Logout (data-test-id='sign-out-button')
4. Verify POST /revoke/token returns HTTP 200 (it does - logout flow itself works)
5. From the same browser, fetch POST /graphql with body { query: '{ me { id userName } }' } and Authorization: Bearer <captured_token>
6. Observe response body
bugs.expected: Storefront /graphql returns 401 Unauthorized OR returns 200 with { me: { userName: 'Anonymous' } }, because the access token was revoked at step 4.
bugs.actual: Storefront /graphql returns HTTP 200 with { data: { me: { id: 'c994fa34-...', userName: 'qa-agent-slot1@virtocommerce.com' } } } - full user data exposed via revoked token. Verified the storefront proxy honors the Bearer header (bogus token returns 'Anonymous'), so the access_token itself is being accepted by the backend after revocation.
networkErrors: POST /graphql returns 200 with authenticated user data when Bearer = revoked access_token
bugs.notes: May be by-design OAuth behavior (only refresh tokens revoked; access tokens valid until natural ~30-min TTL). Suite 044 NOT_TESTED note already flags 'token expiry not exposed in QA'. Needs architectural decision: either explicit token blacklist on /revoke or document the access_token TTL window as an accepted security trade-off. If accepted, BL-AUTH-003 wording 'token revocation must be immediate' should be updated to reflect refresh-token revocation only.

bugs.title: Login on behalf (impersonation) lands on /403 - admin permission or session collision
bugs.failedAssertion: [STATE] Storefront opens in customer context (not admin)
bugs.stepsToReproduce:
1. Open Admin SPA at https://vcst-qa.govirto.com, log in as admin / Password1!
2. Navigate Contacts -> search 'qa-agent-slot1' -> click Agent Chrome contact
3. Click Accounts widget -> click qa-agent-slot1@virtocommerce.com row
4. In the user detail blade, click 'Login on behalf' toolbar action
5. Observe new storefront tab
bugs.expected: Storefront opens in the impersonated user's context (account dashboard or /), with an impersonation banner including the impersonated user's name and a 'Stop Impersonation' control.
bugs.actual: Storefront opens at /account/impersonate/c994fa34-dab9-4238-9c39-28756b3e547d, immediately redirects to /403 'Access denied - You do not have permissions to access the requested page'. Header shows 'Agent Chrome' with the customer's address and cart count, suggesting partial context switch occurred but the destination route rejected.
bugs.notes: Two candidate root causes: (a) admin account lacks an impersonation scope/permission in QA env (Identity policy mismatch on /account/impersonate route); (b) single-browser-context cookie collision (storefront already had an active session for the SAME user). Cannot disambiguate in single-browser test runner. Recommendation: re-run from clean incognito context OR with a target user who is NOT currently signed in to the storefront.
